It was an ancient honor for Israel that they were a people who prayed. Jehoahaz, their king, in his distress, asked the Lord for help; he sought assistance, but not from the idols; what help could they give him? He sought the Lord. See how quick God is to show mercy; how ready to hear prayer; how willing to find a reason to be gracious; otherwise he would not look so far back as the ancient covenant that Israel had so often broken and forfeited. Let this invite and encourage us forever to turn to him, and encourage even those who have forsaken him to return and repent; for there is forgiveness with him, so that he may be feared. And if the Lord answers a mere cry of distress for temporary relief, much more will he regard the prayer of faith for spiritual blessings.

Jehoash, the king, came to Elisha to receive his dying counsel and blessing. It may benefit our spiritual lives to attend the sickbeds and deathbeds of good men, so that we may be encouraged in religion by the comfort they have from it in a dying hour. Elisha assured the king of his success; yet he must look up to God for direction and strength; he must recognize that his own abilities are not enough, but continue on, depending upon divine aid. The trembling hands of the dying prophet, as they showed the power of God, gave this arrow more force than the hands of the king in his full strength. By disregarding the sign, the king lost what it signified, to the grief of the dying prophet. It is troubling to good men to see those to whom they wish well forsake their own mercies and lose advantages against spiritual enemies.

God has many ways to punish a provoking people. Trouble comes sometimes from where we least feared it. The mention of this invasion at the death of Elisha shows that the removal of God’s faithful prophets is a sign of coming judgments. His dead body was a means of giving life to another dead body. This miracle confirmed his prophecies. And it may refer to Christ, by whose death and burial, the grave has been made a safe and happy passage to life for all believers. Jehoash was successful against the Syrians just as often as he struck the ground with the arrows; then a stop was put to his victories. Many have repented too late of distrusts and the narrowness of their desires.
